channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: At least 32 killed, 700 injured in 2 major earthquakes in Venezuela, says acting president
item.description: A 7.2-magnitude earthquake and a 7.5-magnitude were less than a minute apart, said the U.S. Geological Survey. The second earthquake was the largest to hit the country since 1900.
item.content:encoded:
A 7.2-magnitude earthquake and a 7.5-magnitude were less than a minute apart, said the U.S. Geological Survey. The second earthquake was the largest to hit the country since 1900.

(Image credit: Juan Barreto)
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman

item.title: Greetings from sweltering Switzerland
item.description: On the waterfront in Lucerne, Switzerland, soccer fans watched jumbo TVs showing a World Cup match played an ocean away. But the air felt more like the tropics.
item.content:encoded: On the waterfront in Lucerne, Switzerland, soccer fans watched jumbo TVs showing a World Cup match played an ocean away. But the air felt more like the tropics.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: U.N. nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran, but Iran says only after final deal
item.description: The head of the U.N.'s atomic agency said Iran's nuclear enrichment sites would be inspected as part of the interim deal. Iran insists any such visit would only come after a final deal.
item.content:encoded:
The head of the U.N.'s atomic agency said Iran's nuclear enrichment sites would be inspected as part of the interim deal. Iran insists any such visit would only come after a final deal.

(Image credit: Kyodo News)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Inside the gold-mining town where the Ebola outbreak likely started
item.description: NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.
item.content:encoded:
NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.

(Image credit: Arsène Mpiana for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone

item.title: As U.S. government looks to leverage food aid, producers seek other delivery methods
item.description: The U.S. is changing the way food aid is distributed to starving children around the world. It is now using aid as a diplomatic tool as nutrition bars pile up in U.S. factories.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. is changing the way food aid is distributed to starving children around the world. It is now using aid as a diplomatic tool as nutrition bars pile up in U.S. factories.
item.dc:creator: Grant Blankenship

item.title: Archaeologists find huge Viking textile production site in Denmark
item.description: Archaeologists have discovered a huge Viking Age textile production site in Denmark that dates back more than 1,000 years and underlines the sophistication of Viking society.
item.content:encoded:
Archaeologists have discovered a huge Viking Age textile production site in Denmark that dates back more than 1,000 years and underlines the sophistication of Viking society.

(Image credit: James Brooks)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Afghan Taliban hold first, closed-door talks with EU on deportations
item.description: Rights groups criticized the meeting, saying it undermines the EU's human rights obligations.
item.content:encoded:
Rights groups criticized the meeting, saying it undermines the EU's human rights obligations.

(Image credit: Thanassis Stavrakis)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. lifts Iran oil sanctions. And, federal judge rules SAVE voter tool unlawful
item.description: The U.S. has temporarily lifted oil sanctions on Iran as peace talks continue. And, a federal judge ruled that the Trump administration's data system, known as SAVE, is unlawful.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. has temporarily lifted oil sanctions on Iran as peace talks continue. And, a federal judge ruled that the Trump administration's data system, known as SAVE, is unlawful.

(Image credit: Nathan Howard)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Who would pay for Trump's proposed $300 billion Iran reconstruction fund?
item.description: It's still unclear who would fund a proposed $300 billion reconstruction plan for Iran. Former National Security Adviser Jake Sullivan called the whole approach "something entirely new."
item.content:encoded:
It's still unclear who would fund a proposed $300 billion reconstruction plan for Iran. Former National Security Adviser Jake Sullivan called the whole approach "something entirely new."

(Image credit: Nathan Howard)
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: A U.S.-Iran dispute over nuclear inspections clouds work to finalize a war-ending deal
item.description: As U.S.-Iran talks continued, a break in the shipping bottleneck through the Strait of Hormuz appeared to be in the works.
item.content:encoded:
As U.S.-Iran talks continued, a break in the shipping bottleneck through the Strait of Hormuz appeared to be in the works.

(Image credit: Anjum Naveed)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
